Event details

item.title: Make a difference for cats and dogs in need!
item.description:
Sunday, March 8, 2026 at 12 AM

Location: Pawsitive Alliance, 14150 NE 20th St. F1-233, Bellevue, WA

Are you interested in using your time, talent, and energy to support the vision of a healthy and happy home for every cat and dog in Washington? If yes, then please consider joining our Board! Pawsitive Alliance, a volunteer driven tax-exempt nonprofit organization, is having an open call for Board Members. Founded in 2005, the mission of Pawsitive Alliance is to help end the euthanasia of adoptable dogs and cats in Washington by increasing adoptions, supporting spay and neuter programs, and improving pet retention. Pawsitive Alliance advances are mission through our three programs: -  Adoption program: \#WhyNotMePets campaign, highlighting long-term homeless pets, and supporting adoption events to help homeless finds homes -  Spay/Neuter: providing vouchers and financial and logistical support to increase surgeries and access to end pet overpopulation and suffering -  Pet retention: collecting and distributing pet food and supplies and offering pet ID tags and microchips to keep pets thriving with their caretakers Our programs and activities prioritize increasing services for pets, people, and places across Washington state with few resources. Pawsitive Alliance’s biggest strengths are marketing, organizing, fundraising, and collaborating.  We work with more than 50 human and animal-welfare partners each year. Since 2005, we have helped over 8,000 animals find homes, delivered over 170,000 pounds of donated pet food, and facilitated more than 11,000 spay and neuter surgeries. The Pawsitive Alliance Board is a hands-on group of committed individuals, investing their time and resources to support our mission.  As we celebrate our 20th anniversary in 2025, and look to the future, we are looking to add individuals from diverse backgrounds to join the Board and help the organization’s continued success and growth.  Ideal candidates are committed to our mission, can commit at least 10-15 per month to the organization, are able to represent the organization in the community, are ready to engage in strategy setting, fundraising and networking, and bring a well-rounded set of skills to a unique and hard-working team.​ Pawsitive Alliance is always interested in bringing greater diversity and new areas of expertise, energy, and skills to the Board. For more information on the organization, refer to our website at www.pawsitivealliance.org.  Please fill out our Board Application if you share our passion for helping to end pet homelessness in Washington and are interested in serving and helping end pet overpopulation. Those interested in applying should email our Board Recruitment Committee at info@pawsitivealliance.org.

From SEAtoday - All Events

Event details

item.title: Woodinville Half
item.description:
Sunday, March 8, 2026 at 8 AM

Location: Kirkland Marina Park, 25 Lakeshore Plz Dr, Kirkland, WA

Kirk-Wood Half is now the Woodinville Half Hello runners, We know many of you have been looking forward to the Kirk-Wood Half Marathon, and we appreciate your understanding as we share an important update regarding the event. Unfortunately, we have had to change the route for the Kirk-Wood Half due to ongoing construction on the Kirkland Cross Corridor Trail. The good news: the half marathon will still take place on Sunday, March 8 — just with an updated route. The event will now start and finish at Wilmot Park, which was originally planned as the finish line. This eliminates the need for participant shuttles and allows us to offer a flat, fast, and fun course. We are incredibly grateful for the support and flexibility shown by the City of Woodinville and King County Parks in making this possible. At this time, our focus is on delivering an exceptional race experience on March 8. The City of Kirkland has indicated a willingness to permit the event on a future date, and we are exploring the possibility of hosting the Kirk-Wood Half Marathon later this year. Important Updates: The event will now be called the Woodinville Half Marathon. All participants will receive a free slice of pizza from The Pizza Coup post-race . All participants will still receive a full-zip hooded sweatshirt with registration. We will be updating the design. View the new course map: https://www.mapmyrun.com/routes/view/6667331427 Thank you for your continued support, we can’t wait to see you at the start line! Sincerely, Grant Harrington Owner Snohomish Running Company

From SEAtoday - All Events

item.title: WA Concealed Carry Class at Wildlife Committee of
item.description:
Sunday, March 8, 2026 at 9 AM

Location: Wildlife Committee of Washington - WCWShootingSports - Kenmore Range, 1031 228th St SW, Bothell, WA

This 4-hour comprehensive training course is designed to educate individuals on the laws and regulations surrounding the carrying of concealed firearms. This class covers essential topics such as the use of force, firearms safety, and the proper handling and storage of firearms, as well as the specific laws and any requirements for obtaining permits in Washington, Oregon, Arizona, and Utah. Upon completion of the class, attendees will be eligible to apply for the above-mentioned permits. You will also receive a WA - HB-1143 Gun Safety Certificate. DATES and TIMES are subject to change, please view our website for the most up to date and accurate class info. https://www.legalheat.com/classes/in-person/concealed-carry-class-at-wildlife-committee-of-washington-bothell-wa/?utm\_source=facebook&utm\_medium=event

This class covers essential topics such as the use of force, firearms safety, and the proper handling and storage of firearms, as well as the specific laws and any requirements for obtaining permits in Washington, Oregon, Arizona, and Utah. Upon completion of the class, attendees will be eligible to apply for the above-mentioned permits. You will also receive a WA - HB-1143 Gun Safety Certificate. DATES and TIMES are subject to change, please view our website for the most up to date and accurate class info. https://www.legalheat.com/classes/in-person/concealed-carry-class-at-wildlife-committee-of-washington-bothell-wa/?utm\_source=facebook&utm\_medium=event

item.title: Sermon Series: 1 Peter
item.description:
Sunday, March 8, 2026 at 9:30 AM

This winter, from January to Easter, we are walking through 1 Peter at Woodmark Church. Some of you know the book well. For others, it may feel unfamiliar. But Peter names the tension every believer feels. We live in a world that often moves in a different direction. He calls us “exiles,” yet reminds us we are chosen, loved, held by a living hope. The heart of this series is simple. It is not just about studying a letter. It is about becoming the kind of people Peter envisions: Steady. Courageous. Formed by grace. Join us each Sunday, 9:30 am and 11:30 am in Kirkland.

item.title: Toddler Time
item.description:
Sunday, March 8, 2026 at 9:30 AM

Location: Seattle Aquarium, 1483 Alaskan Way, Pier 59, Seattle, WA

Toddler Time is a drop-in program free with Aquarium admission that offers a variety of learner-centered, developmentally age-appropriate, hands-on activities for young children and their grown-ups. Join us for crafts, songs, story time and more. No reservations needed! Feel free to stay for any length of time between 9:30am and noon on select Sundays and Mondays. More information, including potential allergens and sensory warnings for each upcoming date and theme, is available on our website: https://www.seattleaquarium.org/explore-the-aquarium/programs/toddler-time

item.title: Indigenous Peoples Community Day
item.description:
Sunday, March 8, 2026 at 9:30 AM

Location: Seattle Aquarium, 1483 Alaskan Way, Pier 59, Seattle, WA

We are proud to be cohosting this event with the Muckleshoot Indian Tribe! Join us as we welcome members of Indigenous communities to the Aquarium for a day of cultural learning, storytelling and knowledge sharing. This event is open to all members of Indigenous communities and those eager to celebrate and learn more about Indigenous cultures. Explore our habitats and learn about career paths from Aquarium staff. Enjoy cultural demonstrations from local tribes, plus tales from Indigenous storytellers. Grab your tickets for this exciting day of learning and celebration at the Aquarium. Remember, one ticket grants you access to all three Aquarium buildings, plus the day’s activities. Grab yours: https://www.seattleaquarium.org/events/indigenous-peoples-community-day

item.title: TADAIMA: I'm Home
item.description:
Sunday, March 8, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Museum of History and Industry, 860 Terry Ave N, Seattle, Seattle, WA

After more than 80 years, a treasured collection returns “home” to Seattle’s Japanese American community. In 1942 dozens of traditional Boys’ and Girls’ Day dolls were entrusted to Bailey Gatzert Elementary School by Japanese American families facing forced incarceration. Most were never reclaimed after the war. Artist Miya Sukune uses recent research in MOHAI’s collection, as well as interviews with Japanese American survivors and their descendants, to create an installation called “Tadaima,” meaning “I’m home.” TADAIMA: “I’m Home!” Bringing Seattle’s Girls’ and Boys’ Day Dolls Home reconnects the dolls with their traditional role of protecting and blessing children. It invites visitors to celebrate the resilience, memory, and enduring traditions of the Japanese American Community.

From SEAtoday - All Events

Event details

item.title: Samantha Yun Wall: What We Leave Behind
item.description:
Sunday, March 8, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Seattle Art Museum, 1300 First Avenue, Seattle, WA

Samantha Yun Wall’s new paintings use overlapping silhouettes of female figures as portals to unknown spaces and different temporal realms. Impetus for the new body of work is a Korean folk tale in which the Pasque flower is symbolic of a grandmother who passed away without the loving care of her grandchildren. It is a story of melancholy, loss, and remembrance. The delicate hair on the flower’s stem differentiates it, and the artist gives the plant a surreal aspect in some of her paintings, replacing the flower’s center with a watchful eye.

item.title: Anila Quayyum Agha: Geometry of Light
item.description:
Sunday, March 8, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Seattle Art Museum, 1300 First Avenue, Seattle, WA

Anila Quayyum Agha: Geometry of Light invites you to become part of the art. Agha, a Pakistani American artist, animates spaces with her large-scale sculptural installations. Suspended from the ceiling, Agha’s steel cubes are laser-cut with intricate designs that project geometric shadows onto the visitor. She draws on both the light and dark of her own life, using South Asian art practices to convey the gender discrimination she faced growing up as a young girl in Pakistan. An in-depth exploration of Islamic architecture, art, and identity, this is the first solo exhibition of a Pakistani American artist in SAM’s 90-year history. Agha is carving a name out for herself in the art world—visit the Seattle Asian Art Museum for an immersive and illuminating experience.

From SEAtoday - All Events

Event details

item.title: Kameelah Janan Rasheed: we leak, we exceed
item.description:
Sunday, March 8, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Henry Art Gallery, 15th Ave NE & 41st St, Seattle, WA

Kameelah Janan Rasheed \(b. 1985, East Palo Alto, CA; lives and works in Brooklyn, NY\) is a self-described “learner” who creates multi-media works that explore the poetics-pleasures-politics of Black knowledge production, information technologies, \[un\]learning, and belief formation. She often integrates found images and words alongside her own writing to create collaged wall installations and experimental video works. Across her work, Rasheed investigates the revision and life cycles of text, embracing expanded and embodied forms of writing and reading. we leak, we exceed will activate the unique volume and multiple vantage points of the Henry’s double-height gallery, drawing together threads from physics, Black critical thought, and information theory to create an immersive environment that interrogates the spatial and social implications of compression. A common process used in data storage, spatial organization, and information systems, compression abbreviates and collapses complex ideas into more simplified forms. Rasheed questions the way compression comes at the cost of nuance and creates unrecoverable losses. She draws parallels between the compression of information and the containment of people, both physically and through the structuring and defining of identities. Through a network of video, sound, and architectural mark-making, Rasheed proposes alternatively what she calls “an embrace of Black excess and expansion” as a liberatory practice.

item.title: Intro to Improv Eastside - Issaquah SUNDAYS
item.description:
Sunday, March 8, 2026 at 11 AM

Location: Unexpected Productions, 1428 Post Alley, Seattle, WA

Discover improv in Issaquah. Build confidence, meet new people, and learn to create stories through fun, supportive games and scene work. Looking for a fun, fresh way to connect with others, spark creativity, and step out of the everyday routine? Welcome to Intro to Improv at Unexpected Productions Improv School Eastside in Issaquah. Whether you are a working professional hoping to improve communication, a creative soul searching for a new outlet, or someone simply wanting more laughter and community in your week, this class is the perfect place to begin. In this beginner-friendly course, you will learn the foundational skills of improvisation: active listening, collaboration, spontaneity, and using your own life experiences to create honest, engaging scenes and stories. Improv is not about being funny, it is about being present, supportive, and open. And yes, along the way, you will laugh more than you have in ages. Our community values connection and growth, and this class is built on those principles. You will work in a supportive group where the guiding philosophy is simple: When your scene partner looks good, you look good. Together, we build trust, confidence, and creativity, one scene at a time. Under the guidance of our experienced faculty, you will learn to: Be a better listener Conquer fears and build confidence Spark creativity Meet new people and make real connections Become a better problem solver Say yes more in your life Step out of your comfort zone Improve public speaking Fail easier \(and laugh about it\) Discover more about yourself Have an absolute blast This class includes lots of laughter, lots of stage time, and zero pressure. Every student begins here. No experience needed. Just bring curiosity and a willingness to try something new. Unexpected Productions Improv School is thrilled to bring our newest Eastside improv classes to Issaquah, serving the communities of the Sammamish Plateau, East Renton, Bellevue, Snoqualmie Valley, and beyond. Class Details Dates: Sundays for eight \(8\) weeks, from March 8 to May 3, \(No class on April 5th\) Time: 11:00 AM to 1:00pm Location: Improv School Eastside Issaquah 1315 NW Mall Street, Suite 4 Issaquah, WA 98027 \(next to the Gaslamp Bar & Grill\) Maximum students: 16 Ages: 18 plus Cost: $320 Prerequisite: None Note: Attendance at the first session is mandatory, as it sets the tone and foundation for the entire quarter. If you know you will miss the first class, please register for a future session. What students say “You are learning more about improv, more about yourself, making interesting friends, while you are learning and stretching in ways that will make life better both at work and socially, yet everyone is smiling so hard your face hurts. How many stars is that worth?” Jonathan A., 5 Stars on Yelp Cancellation policy There are no refunds.

Event details

item.title: Young Dragon: A Bruce Lee Story
item.description:
Sunday, March 8, 2026 at 11 AM

Location: Seattle Children's Theatre, 201 Thomas Street, Seattle, WA

Before he was a legend, Bruce Lee was just a young man trying to figure it all out—right here in Seattle. This action-packed, soul-searching story follows the Young Dragon as he navigates a new country and finds his voice. Through movement, magic, and heart, we meet the human behind the icon—flawed, fierce, and full of possibility. Watch as Lee learns to clear his brain by mimicking the white noise of water and begins fusing martial arts with philosophy. From street fights in Hong Kong to teaching self-defense and chasing big dreams, Bruce’s journey reminds us that finding your way means first figuring out who you are. Come see how Seattle shaped him into a flexible, fluid, and flowing master—and how he reshaped the world.

From SEAtoday - All Events

Event details

item.title: Boren Banner Series: Camille Trautman
item.description:
Sunday, March 8, 2026 at 11 AM

Location: Frye Art Museum, 704 Terry Ave, Seattle, WA

Every photograph is a reminder that the act of framing is never neutral. In Camille Trautman’s first solo museum exhibition in their hometown, the Seattle-born Duwamish artist uses photography and video to challenge colonial narratives and counter Indigenous erasure. The exhibition presents selections from their ongoing series The North American LCD—spectral self-portraits staged in varied natural landscapes. Rooted in Trautman’s process of coming out as transgender, the images show the artist’s body partially obscured by large LCD screens. The series examines how both landscape photography and digital media can shape or distort cultural identity, often by promoting or denying visibility.

Event details

item.title: Intro to Improv Eastside - Issaquah SUNDAYS
item.description:
Sunday, March 8, 2026 at 11 AM

Location: Unexpected Productions, 1428 Post Alley, Seattle, WA

Discover improv in Issaquah. Build confidence, meet new people, and learn to create stories through fun, supportive games and scene work. Looking for a fun, fresh way to connect with others, spark creativity, and step out of the everyday routine? Welcome to Intro to Improv at Unexpected Productions Improv School Eastside in Issaquah. Whether you are a working professional hoping to improve communication, a creative soul searching for a new outlet, or someone simply wanting more laughter and community in your week, this class is the perfect place to begin. In this beginner-friendly course, you will learn the foundational skills of improvisation: active listening, collaboration, spontaneity, and using your own life experiences to create honest, engaging scenes and stories. Improv is not about being funny, it is about being present, supportive, and open. And yes, along the way, you will laugh more than you have in ages. Our community values connection and growth, and this class is built on those principles. You will work in a supportive group where the guiding philosophy is simple: When your scene partner looks good, you look good. Together, we build trust, confidence, and creativity, one scene at a time. Under the guidance of our experienced faculty, you will learn to: Be a better listener Conquer fears and build confidence Spark creativity Meet new people and make real connections Become a better problem solver Say yes more in your life Step out of your comfort zone Improve public speaking Fail easier \(and laugh about it\) Discover more about yourself Have an absolute blast This class includes lots of laughter, lots of stage time, and zero pressure. Every student begins here. No experience needed. Just bring curiosity and a willingness to try something new. Unexpected Productions Improv School is thrilled to bring our newest Eastside improv classes to Issaquah, serving the communities of the Sammamish Plateau, East Renton, Bellevue, Snoqualmie Valley, and beyond. Class Details Dates: Sundays for eight \(8\) weeks, from March 8 to May 3, \(No class on April 5th\) Time: 11:00 AM to 1:00pm Location: Improv School Eastside Issaquah 1315 NW Mall Street, Suite 4 Issaquah, WA 98027 \(next to the Gaslamp Bar & Grill\) Maximum students: 16 Ages: 18 plus Cost: $320 Prerequisite: None Note: Attendance at the first session is mandatory, as it sets the tone and foundation for the entire quarter. If you know you will miss the first class, please register for a future session. What students say “You are learning more about improv, more about yourself, making interesting friends, while you are learning and stretching in ways that will make life better both at work and socially, yet everyone is smiling so hard your face hurts. How many stars is that worth?” Jonathan A., 5 Stars on Yelp Cancellation policy There are no refunds.

item.description:
Sunday, March 8, 2026 at 11 AM

Come work on bikes on Sundays! Please call/text ahead \(323.926.8054\) to confirm ToDos Bike Garage will be open on a given Sunday. Volunteer run. ToDos Bike Garage is open for bike repair, tune-up, and flat fixes. We have all the basic bike tools and are ready to work on bikes together! We're happy to teach you how to maintain your bike, and help you get it working right. It's free to come work on your bike, and we do have some parts available, but you may need to buy your own parts if something comes up. Follow @todos\_garage on Instagram!

Event details

item.title: Make a difference for cats and dogs in need!
item.description:
Monday, March 9, 2026 at 12 AM

Location: Pawsitive Alliance, 14150 NE 20th St. F1-233, Bellevue, WA

Are you interested in using your time, talent, and energy to support the vision of a healthy and happy home for every cat and dog in Washington? If yes, then please consider joining our Board! Pawsitive Alliance, a volunteer driven tax-exempt nonprofit organization, is having an open call for Board Members. Founded in 2005, the mission of Pawsitive Alliance is to help end the euthanasia of adoptable dogs and cats in Washington by increasing adoptions, supporting spay and neuter programs, and improving pet retention. Pawsitive Alliance advances are mission through our three programs: -  Adoption program: \#WhyNotMePets campaign, highlighting long-term homeless pets, and supporting adoption events to help homeless finds homes -  Spay/Neuter: providing vouchers and financial and logistical support to increase surgeries and access to end pet overpopulation and suffering -  Pet retention: collecting and distributing pet food and supplies and offering pet ID tags and microchips to keep pets thriving with their caretakers Our programs and activities prioritize increasing services for pets, people, and places across Washington state with few resources. Pawsitive Alliance’s biggest strengths are marketing, organizing, fundraising, and collaborating.  We work with more than 50 human and animal-welfare partners each year. Since 2005, we have helped over 8,000 animals find homes, delivered over 170,000 pounds of donated pet food, and facilitated more than 11,000 spay and neuter surgeries. The Pawsitive Alliance Board is a hands-on group of committed individuals, investing their time and resources to support our mission.  As we celebrate our 20th anniversary in 2025, and look to the future, we are looking to add individuals from diverse backgrounds to join the Board and help the organization’s continued success and growth.  Ideal candidates are committed to our mission, can commit at least 10-15 per month to the organization, are able to represent the organization in the community, are ready to engage in strategy setting, fundraising and networking, and bring a well-rounded set of skills to a unique and hard-working team.​ Pawsitive Alliance is always interested in bringing greater diversity and new areas of expertise, energy, and skills to the Board. For more information on the organization, refer to our website at www.pawsitivealliance.org.  Please fill out our Board Application if you share our passion for helping to end pet homelessness in Washington and are interested in serving and helping end pet overpopulation. Those interested in applying should email our Board Recruitment Committee at info@pawsitivealliance.org.

From SEAtoday - All Events

item.title: Toddler Time
item.description:
Monday, March 9, 2026 at 9:30 AM

Location: Seattle Aquarium, 1483 Alaskan Way, Pier 59, Seattle, WA

Toddler Time is a drop-in program free with Aquarium admission that offers a variety of learner-centered, developmentally age-appropriate, hands-on activities for young children and their grown-ups. Join us for crafts, songs, story time and more. No reservations needed! Feel free to stay for any length of time between 9:30am and noon on select Sundays and Mondays. More information, including potential allergens and sensory warnings for each upcoming date and theme, is available on our website: https://www.seattleaquarium.org/explore-the-aquarium/programs/toddler-time

Also shop online at christysports.com or sturtevants.com. Upgrade your gear locally this spring—visit Sturtevant's Bellevue or Tacoma today. Event details

item.title: TADAIMA: I'm Home
item.description:
Monday, March 9, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Museum of History and Industry, 860 Terry Ave N, Seattle, Seattle, WA

After more than 80 years, a treasured collection returns “home” to Seattle’s Japanese American community. In 1942 dozens of traditional Boys’ and Girls’ Day dolls were entrusted to Bailey Gatzert Elementary School by Japanese American families facing forced incarceration. Most were never reclaimed after the war. Artist Miya Sukune uses recent research in MOHAI’s collection, as well as interviews with Japanese American survivors and their descendants, to create an installation called “Tadaima,” meaning “I’m home.” TADAIMA: “I’m Home!” Bringing Seattle’s Girls’ and Boys’ Day Dolls Home reconnects the dolls with their traditional role of protecting and blessing children. It invites visitors to celebrate the resilience, memory, and enduring traditions of the Japanese American Community.

Artist Miya Sukune uses recent research in MOHAI’s collection, as well as interviews with Japanese American survivors and their descendants, to create an installation called “Tadaima,” meaning “I’m home.” TADAIMA: “I’m Home!” Bringing Seattle’s Girls’ and Boys’ Day Dolls Home reconnects the dolls with their traditional role of protecting and blessing children. It invites visitors to celebrate the resilience, memory, and enduring traditions of the Japanese American Community.

After more than 80 years, a treasured collection returns “home” to Seattle’s Japanese American community. In 1942 dozens of traditional Boys’ and Girls’ Day dolls were entrusted to Bailey Gatzert Elementary School by Japanese American families facing forced incarceration. Most were never reclaimed after the war. Artist Miya Sukune uses recent research in MOHAI’s collection, as well as interviews with Japanese American survivors and their descendants, to create an installation called “Tadaima,” meaning “I’m home.” TADAIMA: “I’m Home!” Bringing Seattle’s Girls’ and Boys’ Day Dolls Home reconnects the dolls with their traditional role of protecting and blessing children. It invites visitors to celebrate the resilience, memory, and enduring traditions of the Japanese American Community.

Event details

item.title: Susan Meiselas: Crossings
item.description:
Monday, March 9, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: Photographic Center Northwest (PCNW), 900 12th Ave., Seattle, WA

First presented at the Art Institute of Chicago in 1990, this exhibition by Susan Meiselas intertwines documentary photographs of Central America during the 1970s and 1980s \(from Nicaragua and El Salvador\) along with panoramic images from the US/Mexico border taken in 1989. Additional photographs of the US/Mexico border taken in 2018 are also featured in a site-specific gallery installation. The exhibition invites viewers to see immigration in the context of economic and political crises in the region. Today, the work feels newly urgent. US immigration enforcement has expanded through militarization and constant surveillance, with leaders using fear-driven language to divide communities and legitimize brutal forms of control. These photographs ask us to look carefully, to recognize familiar patterns, and to hold on to our shared humanity across the globe. People move in search of safety, possibility, and a better life for their families, which is a universal hope that crosses borders, generations, and time.

item.title: Food and Cooking Through Science
item.description:
Monday, March 9, 2026 at 5 PM

Location: Seattle Central College Continuing Education, 1701 Broadway, Seattle, WA

Gain a deeper insight into what’s going on when you cook and bake! Whether you are a home cook or a professional, you can become a much better cook by learning the science of food and cooking. You'll fall in love with cooking, as well as the beauty of the amazing world we live in. Focus on understanding the fascinating field of food science through a combination of fun and fascinating lectures and demos. Learn to make better choices about cooking methods and techniques, and how to produce better finished products more easily and consistently. Elevate your knowledge about nutrition, how food works, sustainability issues, and the chemistry of cooking and baking. Develop modern techniques using scientific principles. Learn many fundamental concepts and apply them to real-world cooking and baking.

item.title: Winter Improv Student Showcase
item.description:
Monday, March 9, 2026 at 6 PM

Location: Unexpected Productions, 1428 Post Alley, Seattle, WA

Come see the improv stars of the future debut the skills they’ve learned in Unexpected Productions’ Improv School. Join us tonight at Unexpected Productions’ Market Theater for the Winter Student Showcase! Watch as our talented students—ranging from beginner to advanced levels—take the stage to craft spontaneous, engaging, and often hilarious improv scenes right before your eyes. Our 300, 400, and 500-level students will showcase their newly honed skills, giving you an exclusive preview of the next generation of improv stars. You’ll experience a variety of performance styles inspired by UP’s mainstage shows, making for an unpredictable and highly entertaining night. Stick around after the show to meet the performers—you’ll be able to say, “I saw them before they were famous!” 6:00pm: Chad Sunday 300, Bestock 400, Tim 400, Tony 500 8:00pm: Chad Monday 300, Chazz 400, Laurel 400, Liz 500 Don’t miss this chance to support our rising improv stars and enjoy an evening of laughter and surprises. See you there!

item.title: Make a difference for cats and dogs in need!
item.description:
Tuesday, March 10, 2026 at 12 AM

Location: Pawsitive Alliance, 14150 NE 20th St. F1-233, Bellevue, WA

Are you interested in using your time, talent, and energy to support the vision of a healthy and happy home for every cat and dog in Washington? If yes, then please consider joining our Board! Pawsitive Alliance, a volunteer driven tax-exempt nonprofit organization, is having an open call for Board Members. Founded in 2005, the mission of Pawsitive Alliance is to help end the euthanasia of adoptable dogs and cats in Washington by increasing adoptions, supporting spay and neuter programs, and improving pet retention. Pawsitive Alliance advances are mission through our three programs: -  Adoption program: \#WhyNotMePets campaign, highlighting long-term homeless pets, and supporting adoption events to help homeless finds homes -  Spay/Neuter: providing vouchers and financial and logistical support to increase surgeries and access to end pet overpopulation and suffering -  Pet retention: collecting and distributing pet food and supplies and offering pet ID tags and microchips to keep pets thriving with their caretakers Our programs and activities prioritize increasing services for pets, people, and places across Washington state with few resources. Pawsitive Alliance’s biggest strengths are marketing, organizing, fundraising, and collaborating.  We work with more than 50 human and animal-welfare partners each year. Since 2005, we have helped over 8,000 animals find homes, delivered over 170,000 pounds of donated pet food, and facilitated more than 11,000 spay and neuter surgeries. The Pawsitive Alliance Board is a hands-on group of committed individuals, investing their time and resources to support our mission.  As we celebrate our 20th anniversary in 2025, and look to the future, we are looking to add individuals from diverse backgrounds to join the Board and help the organization’s continued success and growth.  Ideal candidates are committed to our mission, can commit at least 10-15 per month to the organization, are able to represent the organization in the community, are ready to engage in strategy setting, fundraising and networking, and bring a well-rounded set of skills to a unique and hard-working team.​ Pawsitive Alliance is always interested in bringing greater diversity and new areas of expertise, energy, and skills to the Board. For more information on the organization, refer to our website at www.pawsitivealliance.org.  Please fill out our Board Application if you share our passion for helping to end pet homelessness in Washington and are interested in serving and helping end pet overpopulation. Those interested in applying should email our Board Recruitment Committee at info@pawsitivealliance.org.

Pawsitive Alliance’s biggest strengths are marketing, organizing, fundraising, and collaborating.  We work with more than 50 human and animal-welfare partners each year. Since 2005, we have helped over 8,000 animals find homes, delivered over 170,000 pounds of donated pet food, and facilitated more than 11,000 spay and neuter surgeries. The Pawsitive Alliance Board is a hands-on group of committed individuals, investing their time and resources to support our mission.  As we celebrate our 20th anniversary in 2025, and look to the future, we are looking to add individuals from diverse backgrounds to join the Board and help the organization’s continued success and growth.  Ideal candidates are committed to our mission, can commit at least 10-15 per month to the organization, are able to represent the organization in the community, are ready to engage in strategy setting, fundraising and networking, and bring a well-rounded set of skills to a unique and hard-working team.​ Pawsitive Alliance is always interested in bringing greater diversity and new areas of expertise, energy, and skills to the Board. For more information on the organization, refer to our website at www.pawsitivealliance.org.  Please fill out our Board Application if you share our passion for helping to end pet homelessness in Washington and are interested in serving and helping end pet overpopulation. Those interested in applying should email our Board Recruitment Committee at info@pawsitivealliance.org.

item.title: Coworking Tuesdays
item.description:
Tuesday, March 10, 2026 at 9:30 AM

Location: Bothell Kenmore Chamber, 10031 Main Street, Suite A, Bothell, WA

Tuesday Cowork \+ Coffee/Tea \(Weekly\) Need a reason to get out of your inbox cave and into a productive, low-pressure space? Join us every Tuesday for a drop-in coworking morning—bring your laptop, knock out your priorities, and enjoy the energy of working alongside other local professionals. When: Tuesdays | 9:30–11:30 AM | No registration needed Format: Come for the full two hours or pop in when you can Who it’s for: Business owners, remote workers, creatives, students, nonprofit leaders—anyone who wants focused work time \+ light community What to expect: Quiet “get-it-done” work blocks, optional hellos \+ quick intros, and a friendly vibe \(no forced networking\) Location \(through February\): Bothell Visitor Center We’ll rotate locations monthly—same time, same Tuesday rhythm, fresh spaces. Bring: Your laptop \+ whatever you need to work. We have the coffee, tea, and water! Questions? Email sandra@bothellkenmorechamber.org

Also shop online at christysports.com or sturtevants.com. Upgrade your gear locally this spring—visit Sturtevant's Bellevue or Tacoma today. From SEAtoday - All Events

Event details

item.title: TADAIMA: I'm Home
item.description:
Tuesday, March 10, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Museum of History and Industry, 860 Terry Ave N, Seattle, Seattle, WA

After more than 80 years, a treasured collection returns “home” to Seattle’s Japanese American community. In 1942 dozens of traditional Boys’ and Girls’ Day dolls were entrusted to Bailey Gatzert Elementary School by Japanese American families facing forced incarceration. Most were never reclaimed after the war. Artist Miya Sukune uses recent research in MOHAI’s collection, as well as interviews with Japanese American survivors and their descendants, to create an installation called “Tadaima,” meaning “I’m home.” TADAIMA: “I’m Home!” Bringing Seattle’s Girls’ and Boys’ Day Dolls Home reconnects the dolls with their traditional role of protecting and blessing children. It invites visitors to celebrate the resilience, memory, and enduring traditions of the Japanese American Community.

Event details

item.title: Tea with T: Informational Webinar on WEBS
item.description:
Tuesday, March 10, 2026 at 10:30 AM

Description Tea with T: How to Use WEBS Tuesday, March 10 | 10:30 AM PT | Zoom Join GSBA for Tea with T, an informational webinar led by Toraya Miller, where we'll walk through Washington's Electronic Business Solution \(WEBS\) and how to register and use it to find state contracting opportunities. Bring a cup of tea and get set up in 5-10 minutes so bid opportunities that match your commodity codes can start coming straight to your inbox. What is WEBS? Washington's Electronic Business Solution \(WEBS\) is an internet-based bid notification system hosted by the Department of Enterprise Services \(DES\). Many state agencies and educational institutions use WEBS to notify businesses of contracting opportunities based on the commodity codes you select. About Toraya Miller Director of Training & Consulting, GSBA Toraya Miller leads GSBA's training and consulting work, supporting businesses across Washington as they navigate growth, certification, and public-sector opportunities. In this session, Toraya will guide participants through WEBS and share practical tips for getting registered, staying updated, and using the system to identify contracting opportunities with the state. In this webinar, we'll cover: ☕ What WEBS is and why it matters for vendors ☕ How to register as a vendor in WEBS ☕ How to choose and update your commodity codes ☕ Where to find current and upcoming bid opportunities ☕ How to use forecasted purchases to plan ahead ☕ Tips to keep your profile updated so opportunities keep brewing Learn more about WEBS Washington Department of Enterprise Services: Bid Opportunities \+ WEBS

item.title: Susan Meiselas: Crossings
item.description:
Tuesday, March 10, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: Photographic Center Northwest (PCNW), 900 12th Ave., Seattle, WA

First presented at the Art Institute of Chicago in 1990, this exhibition by Susan Meiselas intertwines documentary photographs of Central America during the 1970s and 1980s \(from Nicaragua and El Salvador\) along with panoramic images from the US/Mexico border taken in 1989. Additional photographs of the US/Mexico border taken in 2018 are also featured in a site-specific gallery installation. The exhibition invites viewers to see immigration in the context of economic and political crises in the region. Today, the work feels newly urgent. US immigration enforcement has expanded through militarization and constant surveillance, with leaders using fear-driven language to divide communities and legitimize brutal forms of control. These photographs ask us to look carefully, to recognize familiar patterns, and to hold on to our shared humanity across the globe. People move in search of safety, possibility, and a better life for their families, which is a universal hope that crosses borders, generations, and time.

item.title: Break Even Bottle Program - Every Tuesday at The Cottage!
item.description:
Tuesday, March 10, 2026 at 4 PM

Location: The Cottage, 10029 NE 183rd St, Bothell, WA

Discover something truly exceptional every Tuesday at The Cottage with our Break Even Bottle program, where rare and exquisite spirits are yours to explore. 📅 When: Every Tuesday 4:00 PM to 9:00 PM \(or until the bottle is finished\) 📍 Where: The Cottage – Bothell Each week, we open a unique and rare bottle, offering 1oz pours at cost. It’s your chance to try something extraordinary that you won’t find on most menus—perfect for seasoned connoisseurs and curious tasters alike. Why Join Us? ✨ Exclusive access to rare and premium spirits ✨ Affordable 1oz pours ✨ A cozy and inviting atmosphere Bring your friends and savor an evening of exceptional flavors. Don’t miss out on this unique tasting experience! Reservations are encouraged: https://resy.com/cities/bothell-wa/venues/the-cottage-bothell?date=2025-01-15&seats=2 Spread the word and join us for the Break Even Bottle program at The Cottage. See you Tuesday!

item.title: Baking Fundamentals: Cookies, Cakes, Custards and More
item.description:
Tuesday, March 10, 2026 at 5 PM

Location: Seattle Central College Continuing Education, 1701 Broadway, Seattle, WA

Would you like to be able to make a variety of beautiful and delicious baked treats with confidence? Gain fundamental skills and knowledge for baking in this hands-on class. We will learn about ingredients, interactions, and techniques, using a variety of mixing methods, recipes, and best practices. And we will make a broad range of yummy foods, such as muffins, biscuits, sponges, chiffons, angel food cakes, quiche, pâte à choux, custards, crèmes, pastry doughs, pies, tarts, layer cakes, frostings, meringue, buttercreams, and specialty desserts. Enjoy making delectable baked goods, both sweet and savory. We will also discuss alternative ingredients for people with restrictive diets, food sensitivities, and food allergies. Take advantage of the large, well-equipped kitchen at Seattle Central's Culinary Academy. Learn some of the most useful tips from an experienced chef, and develop practices for working in a clean, safe, and efficient way.

Event details

item.title: Make a difference for cats and dogs in need!
item.description:
Wednesday, March 11, 2026 at 12 AM

Location: Pawsitive Alliance, 14150 NE 20th St. F1-233, Bellevue, WA

Are you interested in using your time, talent, and energy to support the vision of a healthy and happy home for every cat and dog in Washington? If yes, then please consider joining our Board! Pawsitive Alliance, a volunteer driven tax-exempt nonprofit organization, is having an open call for Board Members. Founded in 2005, the mission of Pawsitive Alliance is to help end the euthanasia of adoptable dogs and cats in Washington by increasing adoptions, supporting spay and neuter programs, and improving pet retention. Pawsitive Alliance advances are mission through our three programs: -  Adoption program: \#WhyNotMePets campaign, highlighting long-term homeless pets, and supporting adoption events to help homeless finds homes -  Spay/Neuter: providing vouchers and financial and logistical support to increase surgeries and access to end pet overpopulation and suffering -  Pet retention: collecting and distributing pet food and supplies and offering pet ID tags and microchips to keep pets thriving with their caretakers Our programs and activities prioritize increasing services for pets, people, and places across Washington state with few resources. Pawsitive Alliance’s biggest strengths are marketing, organizing, fundraising, and collaborating.  We work with more than 50 human and animal-welfare partners each year. Since 2005, we have helped over 8,000 animals find homes, delivered over 170,000 pounds of donated pet food, and facilitated more than 11,000 spay and neuter surgeries. The Pawsitive Alliance Board is a hands-on group of committed individuals, investing their time and resources to support our mission.  As we celebrate our 20th anniversary in 2025, and look to the future, we are looking to add individuals from diverse backgrounds to join the Board and help the organization’s continued success and growth.  Ideal candidates are committed to our mission, can commit at least 10-15 per month to the organization, are able to represent the organization in the community, are ready to engage in strategy setting, fundraising and networking, and bring a well-rounded set of skills to a unique and hard-working team.​ Pawsitive Alliance is always interested in bringing greater diversity and new areas of expertise, energy, and skills to the Board. For more information on the organization, refer to our website at www.pawsitivealliance.org.  Please fill out our Board Application if you share our passion for helping to end pet homelessness in Washington and are interested in serving and helping end pet overpopulation. Those interested in applying should email our Board Recruitment Committee at info@pawsitivealliance.org.

From SEAtoday - All Events

item.title: Eastside Coffee Outside Every Wednesday Morning
item.description:
Wednesday, March 11, 2026 at 7 AM

Location: Rotary Central Station Picnic Shelter, 1 Railroad Avenue, Kirkland, WA

Eastside Coffee Outside Every Wednesday Morning Drop by to join us ANY Wednesday, rain or shine, for a chat, a snack, and something hot to drink. No RSVP necessary. You'll find us Every Wednesday, 7:00 AM to 9:30 AM Rotary Central Station \(the picnic shelter\) 1 Railroad Ave, Kirkland, WA 98033 Anna brings her fresh-baked vegan muffins to share. Dylan often brews different varieties of loose-leaf tea to share. Kurt brews regular and decaf coffee and brings hot water, tea bags, and hot cocoa packets. Bring your pup for a treat, too! We're here from 7-9:30am, but you can stop by for as much or as little of that time as works for you. It's on the Cross Kirkland Corridor, so it's easily accessible for walkers, joggers, bikers, and by car. There are reusable mugs available, or bring your own. Hope to see you ANY or EVERY Wednesday morning!

From SEAtoday - All Events

item.title: Bothell Business Forum
item.description:
Wednesday, March 11, 2026 at 8:30 AM

Location: Bothell City Hall, 18415 101st Avenue Northeast, Bothell, WA

RSVP HERE Join the Bothell Business Forum – Connect, Collaborate, & Grow! 🚀 Are you a professional, business owner, or organization leader who does business—or wants to do business—in Bothell? Don’t miss the Bothell Business Forum, where opportunity meets collaboration! ✨ Why Attend? ✔ Build valuable connections with local businesses & professionals ✔ Discuss key issues impacting Bothell’s business community ✔ Explore new partnerships and collaborative opportunities ✔ Exchange resources and share company news ✔ Engage with city officials in a meaningful two-way dialogue 📅 Date: March 11, 2026 📍 Location: Bothell City Hall, 18415 101st Avenue NE, Bothell, WA 🕒 Time: 8:30-9:45 | Doors open at 8:15, program begins promptly at 8:30 📩 RSVP: CLICK HERE FREE to Chamber and non-Chamber members. Don’t miss this chance to expand your network, gain insights, and be part of the thriving Bothell business community!  Spread the word and bring a colleague! We look forward to seeing you there. \#BothellBusiness \#Networking \#BusinessGrowth \#SupportLocal \#BothellChamber

item.title: TADAIMA: I'm Home
item.description:
Wednesday, March 11, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Museum of History and Industry, 860 Terry Ave N, Seattle, Seattle, WA

After more than 80 years, a treasured collection returns “home” to Seattle’s Japanese American community. In 1942 dozens of traditional Boys’ and Girls’ Day dolls were entrusted to Bailey Gatzert Elementary School by Japanese American families facing forced incarceration. Most were never reclaimed after the war. Artist Miya Sukune uses recent research in MOHAI’s collection, as well as interviews with Japanese American survivors and their descendants, to create an installation called “Tadaima,” meaning “I’m home.” TADAIMA: “I’m Home!” Bringing Seattle’s Girls’ and Boys’ Day Dolls Home reconnects the dolls with their traditional role of protecting and blessing children. It invites visitors to celebrate the resilience, memory, and enduring traditions of the Japanese American Community.

From SEAtoday - All Events

Event details

item.title: Get Grant Ready
item.description:
Wednesday, March 11, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: GSBA, 400 E Pine St Ste 322, Seattle, WA

Description Get Grant Ready: Grants Preparedness Workshop for Micro Businesses Join GSBA for a practical Spring workshop focused on helping small and micro businesses break down barriers to securing grants. About This Workshop Facilitated by GSBA's Grant Writer & Manager, Devonshire Lokke, this Spring workshop will focus on breaking down barriers to securing grants for small and micro business. Topics will include grant research \(where to find grants for small businesses\), grant writing \(how to apply for grants\), budgeting \(how to track and manage grant funds\), and reporting \(how to share successes and challenges with funders\). As a grant writer and manager for 6\+ years, Devonshire knows that grants can seem intimidating and overwhelming, especially for a small or micro-business owner. This workshop will provide entrepreneurs with the confidence, tools, and resources to quickly and competitively pursue future grant opportunities \(presented by both GSBA and other funders\). Workshops will be open to GSBA members and non-members and will be in person at the GSBA offices in Capitol Hill, Seattle. The session will conclude with 30 minutes of Q&A, to address participants' unique business needs. What You'll Learn Where to find grant opportunities relevant to small and micro businesses How to read and assess grant requirements before applying Best practices for grant applications and narratives How to build and track grant budgets with confidence What funders expect in grant reporting and follow-up Common pitfalls-and how to avoid them Who Should Attend Micro businesses and sole proprietors Early-stage entrepreneurs Businesses applying for grants for the first time Businesses that have applied before but want stronger results Business owners seeking clear, practical guidance No prior grant experience is required.

From SEAtoday - All Events

Event details

item.title: Samantha Yun Wall: What We Leave Behind
item.description:
Wednesday, March 11, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Seattle Art Museum, 1300 First Avenue, Seattle, WA

Samantha Yun Wall’s new paintings use overlapping silhouettes of female figures as portals to unknown spaces and different temporal realms. Impetus for the new body of work is a Korean folk tale in which the Pasque flower is symbolic of a grandmother who passed away without the loving care of her grandchildren. It is a story of melancholy, loss, and remembrance. The delicate hair on the flower’s stem differentiates it, and the artist gives the plant a surreal aspect in some of her paintings, replacing the flower’s center with a watchful eye.

Event details

item.title: Anila Quayyum Agha: Geometry of Light
item.description:
Wednesday, March 11, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Seattle Art Museum, 1300 First Avenue, Seattle, WA

Anila Quayyum Agha: Geometry of Light invites you to become part of the art. Agha, a Pakistani American artist, animates spaces with her large-scale sculptural installations. Suspended from the ceiling, Agha’s steel cubes are laser-cut with intricate designs that project geometric shadows onto the visitor. She draws on both the light and dark of her own life, using South Asian art practices to convey the gender discrimination she faced growing up as a young girl in Pakistan. An in-depth exploration of Islamic architecture, art, and identity, this is the first solo exhibition of a Pakistani American artist in SAM’s 90-year history. Agha is carving a name out for herself in the art world—visit the Seattle Asian Art Museum for an immersive and illuminating experience.

item.title: Boren Banner Series: Camille Trautman
item.description:
Wednesday, March 11, 2026 at 11 AM

Location: Frye Art Museum, 704 Terry Ave, Seattle, WA

Every photograph is a reminder that the act of framing is never neutral. In Camille Trautman’s first solo museum exhibition in their hometown, the Seattle-born Duwamish artist uses photography and video to challenge colonial narratives and counter Indigenous erasure. The exhibition presents selections from their ongoing series The North American LCD—spectral self-portraits staged in varied natural landscapes. Rooted in Trautman’s process of coming out as transgender, the images show the artist’s body partially obscured by large LCD screens. The series examines how both landscape photography and digital media can shape or distort cultural identity, often by promoting or denying visibility.

From SEAtoday - All Events

item.title: Susan Meiselas: Crossings
item.description:
Wednesday, March 11, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: Photographic Center Northwest (PCNW), 900 12th Ave., Seattle, WA

First presented at the Art Institute of Chicago in 1990, this exhibition by Susan Meiselas intertwines documentary photographs of Central America during the 1970s and 1980s \(from Nicaragua and El Salvador\) along with panoramic images from the US/Mexico border taken in 1989. Additional photographs of the US/Mexico border taken in 2018 are also featured in a site-specific gallery installation. The exhibition invites viewers to see immigration in the context of economic and political crises in the region. Today, the work feels newly urgent. US immigration enforcement has expanded through militarization and constant surveillance, with leaders using fear-driven language to divide communities and legitimize brutal forms of control. These photographs ask us to look carefully, to recognize familiar patterns, and to hold on to our shared humanity across the globe. People move in search of safety, possibility, and a better life for their families, which is a universal hope that crosses borders, generations, and time.

item.title: North End SHG Park Day!
item.description:
Wednesday, March 11, 2026 at 1:30 PM

Location: St Edward State Park, 6990 NE 145th, Kenmore, WA

Welcome to SHG's North End weekly parkday group! We meet every Wednesday during the school year, rain or shine, at St. Edward State Park. The play is unstructured and child-led, and often includes large field games like capture the flag or freeze tag, or smaller groups adventuring around the large playground together. All homeschool ages are welcome, and our current age ranges are usually 5 to 16. Because it is a state park, the parking lot requires a yearly Discover Pass, or a day pass. Annual passes will be increasing from $30 to $45 on October 1st. We want park day to be accessible for all homeschoolers, so if parking costs is a barrier, please contact Christine Jones for options. More information on the park can be found here: https://parks.wa.gov/find-parks/state-parks/saint-edward-state-park Parking passes can be found here: https://store.discoverpass.wa.gov

item.title: Get Grant Ready - VIRTUAL
item.description:
Wednesday, March 11, 2026 at 2 PM

Description Get Grant Ready: Grants Preparedness Workshop for Micro Businesses Virtual - March 10 - 2pm Join GSBA for a practical Spring workshop focused on helping small and micro businesses break down barriers to securing grants. About This Workshop Facilitated by GSBA's Grant Writer & Manager, Devonshire Lokke, this Spring workshop will focus on breaking down barriers to securing grants for small and micro business. Topics will include grant research \(where to find grants for small businesses\), grant writing \(how to apply for grants\), budgeting \(how to track and manage grant funds\), and reporting \(how to share successes and challenges with funders\). As a grant writer and manager for 6\+ years, Devonshire knows that grants can seem intimidating and overwhelming, especially for a small or micro-business owner. This workshop will provide entrepreneurs with the confidence, tools, and resources to quickly and competitively pursue future grant opportunities \(presented by both GSBA and other funders\). Workshops will be open to GSBA members and non-members and will be in person at the GSBA offices in Capitol Hill, Seattle. The session will conclude with 30 minutes of Q&A, to address participants' unique business needs. What You'll Learn Where to find grant opportunities relevant to small and micro businesses How to read and assess grant requirements before applying Best practices for grant applications and narratives How to build and track grant budgets with confidence What funders expect in grant reporting and follow-up Common pitfalls-and how to avoid them Who Should Attend Micro businesses and sole proprietors Early-stage entrepreneurs Businesses applying for grants for the first time Businesses that have applied before but want stronger results Business owners seeking clear, practical guidance No prior grant experience is required.

item.title: Marketing & Branding for Beginners
item.description:
Wednesday, March 11, 2026 at 2 PM

Location: Green River College, Kent Campus, Kent, WA

Description Youth leaders learn to tell their story and represent their vision effectively. This class breaks down marketing and branding into simple, actionable steps-from creating a brand identity to crafting a social media strategy. Students will discover how strong brands inspire trust and attract community support. Key Takeaways:  Understand branding, social media, and marketing strategy.  Learn how to communicate a mission and connect with customers.  Activity: Create a logo, tagline, and one-page marketing plan.

Event details

item.title: Make a difference for cats and dogs in need!
item.description:
Thursday, March 12, 2026 at 12 AM

Location: Pawsitive Alliance, 14150 NE 20th St. F1-233, Bellevue, WA

Are you interested in using your time, talent, and energy to support the vision of a healthy and happy home for every cat and dog in Washington? If yes, then please consider joining our Board! Pawsitive Alliance, a volunteer driven tax-exempt nonprofit organization, is having an open call for Board Members. Founded in 2005, the mission of Pawsitive Alliance is to help end the euthanasia of adoptable dogs and cats in Washington by increasing adoptions, supporting spay and neuter programs, and improving pet retention. Pawsitive Alliance advances are mission through our three programs: -  Adoption program: \#WhyNotMePets campaign, highlighting long-term homeless pets, and supporting adoption events to help homeless finds homes -  Spay/Neuter: providing vouchers and financial and logistical support to increase surgeries and access to end pet overpopulation and suffering -  Pet retention: collecting and distributing pet food and supplies and offering pet ID tags and microchips to keep pets thriving with their caretakers Our programs and activities prioritize increasing services for pets, people, and places across Washington state with few resources. Pawsitive Alliance’s biggest strengths are marketing, organizing, fundraising, and collaborating.  We work with more than 50 human and animal-welfare partners each year. Since 2005, we have helped over 8,000 animals find homes, delivered over 170,000 pounds of donated pet food, and facilitated more than 11,000 spay and neuter surgeries. The Pawsitive Alliance Board is a hands-on group of committed individuals, investing their time and resources to support our mission.  As we celebrate our 20th anniversary in 2025, and look to the future, we are looking to add individuals from diverse backgrounds to join the Board and help the organization’s continued success and growth.  Ideal candidates are committed to our mission, can commit at least 10-15 per month to the organization, are able to represent the organization in the community, are ready to engage in strategy setting, fundraising and networking, and bring a well-rounded set of skills to a unique and hard-working team.​ Pawsitive Alliance is always interested in bringing greater diversity and new areas of expertise, energy, and skills to the Board. For more information on the organization, refer to our website at www.pawsitivealliance.org.  Please fill out our Board Application if you share our passion for helping to end pet homelessness in Washington and are interested in serving and helping end pet overpopulation. Those interested in applying should email our Board Recruitment Committee at info@pawsitivealliance.org.

Event details

item.title: Womxn & Blues: Be What You Want To Be
item.description:
Thursday, March 12, 2026 at 7:30 AM

Location: The Royal Room, 5000 Rainier Ave South, Seattle, WA

Doors 6:30, Show 7:30. Tickets: $20 advance, $25 door Featuring: Amber Flame, Carly T, Havilah Rand & Najah Todd The Blues have long been a rally cry transforming personal and collective hardships – stemming from racial injustice and systemic oppression – into a musical language of resistance and essential expression. Rooted in field hollers and call-and-response, these songs provided emotional resilience and solidarity within Black communities. Continue the journey with us as we explore historic and current ideations of this truly American art form. In the song, Be What You Want To Be, Koko Taylor lays out both the invitation and consequence in living by this declaration. This session’s artists have their own take on the theme.

item.title: SafeCampus Violence Prevention and Response Training
item.description:
Thursday, March 12, 2026 at 9:30 AM

Location: University of Washington, Seattle, WA

Thursday, Mar 12, 2026, 9:30 - 11 a.m. PDT During this interactive training, we ask participants to consider their unique role in preventing violence at the University of Washington. This training has been adapted to consider the remote academic environments that many staff, students, and faculty are currently experiencing. We will explore mental health and warning signs of different forms of violence including; workplace violence, relationship violence, sexual harassment, and stalking. We will briefly practice in-person and remote bystander intervention techniques and build your understanding of UW resources. Click the link below to register for this training. Campus room: Online Zoom Training Accessibility Contact: safecampus@uw.edu Event Types: Information Sessions, Lectures/Seminars, Workshops More info: forms.office.com…

item.title: Sturtevant's Ski-Ya-Later Sale: Up to 50% Off Ski and SnowboardGear
item.description:
Thursday, March 12, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Sturtevant's Ski Mart, 13219 NE 20th St., Bellevue, WA

PNW skiers and snowboarders—spring is the smartest time to upgrade! Sturtevant's \(part of Christy Sports\) is hosting the annual Ski-Ya-Later Sale from March 6-29, offering up to 50% off current-season skis, snowboards, boots, bindings, apparel, and more—perfect as spring storms bring late-season conditions and families plan ahead during spring break. Shop in-person at our Bellevue locations for expert tuning & repair, custom boot fitting, water sports rentals, and more: Sturtevant's – 13131 NE 20th St., Bellevue, WA 98005 \(425.869.7669\) Hours: Mon-Fri 10 am-7 pm, Sat-Sun 10 am- 6 pm Sturtevant's Ski Mart – 13219 NE 20th St., Bellevue, WA 98005 \(425.637.8958\) Hours: Mon-Fri 10 am-7 pm, Sat-Sun 10 am-6 pm Or head to Tacoma: Sturtevant's Ski Mart – 2220 So. 37th St., Tacoma, WA 98409 \(253.473.1134\) Hours: Mon-Fri 10 am-7 pm, Sat-Sun 10 am-6 pm. Great for families: Stop by for kids' fit checks, season rental info, and early purchases before fall pricing. All discounted items are final sale; selection varies by store. Also shop online at christysports.com or sturtevants.com. Upgrade your gear locally this spring—visit Sturtevant's Bellevue or Tacoma today. Dates: March 6 – March 29, 2026 \(Progressive discounts: Up to 30% off March 6-12 with the best selection; up to 40% off March 13-19; up to 50% off March 20-29 for the deepest savings\)

item.title: GSBA Capitol Hill Monthly Safety Call
item.description:
Thursday, March 12, 2026 at 10 AM

Description GSBA: Capitol Hill Safety Call Join GSBA, Jen Carl, and Gabriel Neuman for our monthly GSBA: Capitol Hill Safety Call. Held on Zoom, these virtual conversations provide space for neighborhood updates, safety resources, and open dialogue with local businesses and community partners. item.title: TADAIMA: I'm Home
item.description:
Thursday, March 12, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Museum of History and Industry, 860 Terry Ave N, Seattle, Seattle, WA

After more than 80 years, a treasured collection returns “home” to Seattle’s Japanese American community. In 1942 dozens of traditional Boys’ and Girls’ Day dolls were entrusted to Bailey Gatzert Elementary School by Japanese American families facing forced incarceration. Most were never reclaimed after the war. Artist Miya Sukune uses recent research in MOHAI’s collection, as well as interviews with Japanese American survivors and their descendants, to create an installation called “Tadaima,” meaning “I’m home.” TADAIMA: “I’m Home!” Bringing Seattle’s Girls’ and Boys’ Day Dolls Home reconnects the dolls with their traditional role of protecting and blessing children. It invites visitors to celebrate the resilience, memory, and enduring traditions of the Japanese American Community.

item.title: Samantha Yun Wall: What We Leave Behind
item.description:
Thursday, March 12, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Seattle Art Museum, 1300 First Avenue, Seattle, WA

Samantha Yun Wall’s new paintings use overlapping silhouettes of female figures as portals to unknown spaces and different temporal realms. Impetus for the new body of work is a Korean folk tale in which the Pasque flower is symbolic of a grandmother who passed away without the loving care of her grandchildren. It is a story of melancholy, loss, and remembrance. The delicate hair on the flower’s stem differentiates it, and the artist gives the plant a surreal aspect in some of her paintings, replacing the flower’s center with a watchful eye.

item.title: Anila Quayyum Agha: Geometry of Light
item.description:
Thursday, March 12, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Seattle Art Museum, 1300 First Avenue, Seattle, WA

Anila Quayyum Agha: Geometry of Light invites you to become part of the art. Agha, a Pakistani American artist, animates spaces with her large-scale sculptural installations. Suspended from the ceiling, Agha’s steel cubes are laser-cut with intricate designs that project geometric shadows onto the visitor. She draws on both the light and dark of her own life, using South Asian art practices to convey the gender discrimination she faced growing up as a young girl in Pakistan. An in-depth exploration of Islamic architecture, art, and identity, this is the first solo exhibition of a Pakistani American artist in SAM’s 90-year history. Agha is carving a name out for herself in the art world—visit the Seattle Asian Art Museum for an immersive and illuminating experience.

From SEAtoday - All Events

Event details

item.title: Kameelah Janan Rasheed: we leak, we exceed
item.description:
Thursday, March 12, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Henry Art Gallery, 15th Ave NE & 41st St, Seattle, WA

Kameelah Janan Rasheed \(b. 1985, East Palo Alto, CA; lives and works in Brooklyn, NY\) is a self-described “learner” who creates multi-media works that explore the poetics-pleasures-politics of Black knowledge production, information technologies, \[un\]learning, and belief formation. She often integrates found images and words alongside her own writing to create collaged wall installations and experimental video works. Across her work, Rasheed investigates the revision and life cycles of text, embracing expanded and embodied forms of writing and reading. we leak, we exceed will activate the unique volume and multiple vantage points of the Henry’s double-height gallery, drawing together threads from physics, Black critical thought, and information theory to create an immersive environment that interrogates the spatial and social implications of compression. A common process used in data storage, spatial organization, and information systems, compression abbreviates and collapses complex ideas into more simplified forms. Rasheed questions the way compression comes at the cost of nuance and creates unrecoverable losses. She draws parallels between the compression of information and the containment of people, both physically and through the structuring and defining of identities. Through a network of video, sound, and architectural mark-making, Rasheed proposes alternatively what she calls “an embrace of Black excess and expansion” as a liberatory practice.

item.title: Sturtevant's Ski-Ya-Later Sale: Up to 50% Off Ski and SnowboardGear
item.description:
Thursday, March 12, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Sturtevant's Ski Mart, 13219 NE 20th St., Bellevue, WA

PNW skiers and snowboarders—spring is the smartest time to upgrade! Sturtevant's \(part of Christy Sports\) is hosting the annual Ski-Ya-Later Sale from March 6-29, offering up to 50% off current-season skis, snowboards, boots, bindings, apparel, and more—perfect as spring storms bring late-season conditions and families plan ahead during spring break. Shop in-person at our Bellevue locations for expert tuning & repair, custom boot fitting, water sports rentals, and more: Sturtevant's – 13131 NE 20th St., Bellevue, WA 98005 \(425.869.7669\) Hours: Mon-Fri 10 am-7 pm, Sat-Sun 10 am- 6 pm Sturtevant's Ski Mart – 13219 NE 20th St., Bellevue, WA 98005 \(425.637.8958\) Hours: Mon-Fri 10 am-7 pm, Sat-Sun 10 am-6 pm Or head to Tacoma: Sturtevant's Ski Mart – 2220 So. 37th St., Tacoma, WA 98409 \(253.473.1134\) Hours: Mon-Fri 10 am-7 pm, Sat-Sun 10 am-6 pm. Great for families: Stop by for kids' fit checks, season rental info, and early purchases before fall pricing. All discounted items are final sale; selection varies by store. Also shop online at christysports.com or sturtevants.com. Upgrade your gear locally this spring—visit Sturtevant's Bellevue or Tacoma today. Dates: March 6 – March 29, 2026 \(Progressive discounts: Up to 30% off March 6-12 with the best selection; up to 40% off March 13-19; up to 50% off March 20-29 for the deepest savings\)

Event details

item.title: Boren Banner Series: Camille Trautman
item.description:
Thursday, March 12, 2026 at 11 AM

Location: Frye Art Museum, 704 Terry Ave, Seattle, WA

Every photograph is a reminder that the act of framing is never neutral. In Camille Trautman’s first solo museum exhibition in their hometown, the Seattle-born Duwamish artist uses photography and video to challenge colonial narratives and counter Indigenous erasure. The exhibition presents selections from their ongoing series The North American LCD—spectral self-portraits staged in varied natural landscapes. Rooted in Trautman’s process of coming out as transgender, the images show the artist’s body partially obscured by large LCD screens. The series examines how both landscape photography and digital media can shape or distort cultural identity, often by promoting or denying visibility.

item.description:
Thursday, March 12, 2026 at 11:30 AM

Location: Youngtown Cultural Arts Center, 4408 Delridge Way Sw, Seattle, WA

Attendees will hear clear examples, walk through practical how to moments, and take part in an open forum where questions and curiosities will shape our discussion. It will be an accessible, hands on look at AI that helps West Seattle businesses work smarter, not harder. Bring your questions and your laptops! Explore new horizons and connect with fellow professionals at the West Seattle Chamber&'s monthly luncheons, where engaging discussions and networking opportunities lay the groundwork for you to grow your business.

item.title: Inaugural Albers Marketing Alumni Mentorship Mixer
item.description:
Thursday, March 12, 2026 at 12 PM

Join fellow Seattle University Albers School Marketing alumni for an engaging virtual conversation designed to strengthen connections and support your continued professional growth. This interactive session will feature a facilitated Q&A with industry leaders Katie Milburn \(’14\), Manager of Internal Recruiting at Salesforce, and Lindsey Davis, Senior Manager of Strategy and Operations, People eXperience and Technology \(PXT\) at AWS. Together, they will share insights on career development, leadership, navigating change, and building meaningful professional pathways in today’s evolving workplace. Following the moderated discussion, participants will move into small breakout rooms for more personalized conversations with industry mentors and Albers faculty. This format offers a unique opportunity to ask questions, exchange ideas, and connect with peers in a more informal setting. Whether you are early in your career, considering your next move, or looking to expand your professional network, this event is designed to provide practical advice, fresh perspectives, and renewed connection to the Albers Marketing community. We look forward to seeing you online!

From SEAtoday - All Events

item.title: Restoration Event | Native Plant Nursery
item.description:
Thursday, March 12, 2026 at 12 PM

Monthly Native Plant Nursery Work Party Calling all native plant lovers! We're looking for volunteers to join us each month to help us care for the plants in our native plant nursery. Every year we host a c ouple thousand native plants in the nursery until they're ready to be put in the ground in parks across the island. This will be a great oppor tunity to learn more about native plants, learn about basic plant care, and help us ensure our plants healthy before being put in their new homes. Tasks may include: weeding potted plants, up-potting or repotting native plants, plant organizing, plant propagation, weeding gravel areas, tool care, nursery pot maintenance, and watering. When: Thursday, March 12th, 12pm-2pm \(join us on your lunch break!\) Where: Luther Burbank Park, Caretakers building What to bring: Dress for the weather in clothes you don't mind getting dirty \(think: long pants, boots or sneakers\). Bring water. Small snacks, gloves, and tools will be provided. Registration: https://volunteersignup.org/B4AFB Opens in new w indow PDF Download Word Download Excel Download PowerPoint Dow nload Document Download

From SEAtoday - All Events

Event details

item.title: Susan Meiselas: Crossings
item.description:
Thursday, March 12, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: Photographic Center Northwest (PCNW), 900 12th Ave., Seattle, WA

First presented at the Art Institute of Chicago in 1990, this exhibition by Susan Meiselas intertwines documentary photographs of Central America during the 1970s and 1980s \(from Nicaragua and El Salvador\) along with panoramic images from the US/Mexico border taken in 1989. Additional photographs of the US/Mexico border taken in 2018 are also featured in a site-specific gallery installation. The exhibition invites viewers to see immigration in the context of economic and political crises in the region. Today, the work feels newly urgent. US immigration enforcement has expanded through militarization and constant surveillance, with leaders using fear-driven language to divide communities and legitimize brutal forms of control. These photographs ask us to look carefully, to recognize familiar patterns, and to hold on to our shared humanity across the globe. People move in search of safety, possibility, and a better life for their families, which is a universal hope that crosses borders, generations, and time.

From SEAtoday - All Events

This interactive session will feature a facilitated Q&A with industry leaders Katie Milburn \(’14\), Manager of Internal Recruiting at Salesforce, and Lindsey Davis, Senior Manager of Strategy and Operations, People eXperience and Technology \(PXT\) at AWS. Together, they will share insights on career development, leadership, navigating change, and building meaningful professional pathways in today’s evolving workplace. Following the moderated discussion, participants will move into small breakout rooms for more personalized conversations with industry mentors and Albers faculty. This format offers a unique opportunity to ask questions, exchange ideas, and connect with peers in a more informal setting. Whether you are early in your career, considering your next move, or looking to expand your professional network, this event is designed to provide practical advice, fresh perspectives, and renewed connection to the Albers Marketing community. We look forward to seeing you online!

item.title: Restoration Event | Native Plant Nursery
item.description:
Thursday, March 12, 2026 at 12 PM

Monthly Native Plant Nursery Work Party Calling all native plant lovers! We're looking for volunteers to join us each month to help us care for the plants in our native plant nursery. Every year we host a c ouple thousand native plants in the nursery until they're ready to be put in the ground in parks across the island. This will be a great oppor tunity to learn more about native plants, learn about basic plant care, and help us ensure our plants healthy before being put in their new homes. Tasks may include: weeding potted plants, up-potting or repotting native plants, plant organizing, plant propagation, weeding gravel areas, tool care, nursery pot maintenance, and watering. When: Thursday, March 12th, 12pm-2pm \(join us on your lunch break!\) Where: Luther Burbank Park, Caretakers building What to bring: Dress for the weather in clothes you don't mind getting dirty \(think: long pants, boots or sneakers\). Bring water. Small snacks, gloves, and tools will be provided. Event details

item.title: Susan Meiselas: Crossings
item.description:
Thursday, March 12, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: Photographic Center Northwest (PCNW), 900 12th Ave., Seattle, WA

First presented at the Art Institute of Chicago in 1990, this exhibition by Susan Meiselas intertwines documentary photographs of Central America during the 1970s and 1980s \(from Nicaragua and El Salvador\) along with panoramic images from the US/Mexico border taken in 1989. Additional photographs of the US/Mexico border taken in 2018 are also featured in a site-specific gallery installation. The exhibition invites viewers to see immigration in the context of economic and political crises in the region. Today, the work feels newly urgent. US immigration enforcement has expanded through militarization and constant surveillance, with leaders using fear-driven language to divide communities and legitimize brutal forms of control. These photographs ask us to look carefully, to recognize familiar patterns, and to hold on to our shared humanity across the globe. People move in search of safety, possibility, and a better life for their families, which is a universal hope that crosses borders, generations, and time.
