item.title: Beyond the Cart: Recycling & Reuse Event 2026
item.description:
Saturday, March 21, 2026 at 9 AM

Location: South Seattle Community College, 6000 16th Ave SW, Seattle, WA

Description Have stuff to recycle that doesn't go in your cart? Bring it to the West Seattle Recycling & Reuse Collection event on Saturday, March 21 between 9:00 AM and 12:00 PM! We host this event every year to collect items for recycling and reuse that can't go in your home recycle carts or dumpsters. Events are free and open to all Seattle residents. We heard your feedback and made big changes! This year, you'll park your car in the South Seattle College SOUTH parking lot. Volunteers will be available on site to help anyone with mobility needs. Early and late drop-offs will not be accepted, and trucks leave when full. Beyond the Cart Community Reuse & Recycling Event, brought to you by the West Seattle Chamber and the West Seattle Junction Association, in collaboration with Waste Management and Seattle Public Utilities. ACCEPTED ITEMS: Foam blocks & peanuts Household batteries \(no damaged batteries\) Florescent tubes and bulbs \(no broken bulbs, limit: 4 ft.\) Small, empty propane camping canisters Small electronics \(TVs, computers, etc.\) Small appliances \(non-freon\) Paper for shredding \(limit: 4 boxes of paper\) Clothing, accessories, bedding, and curtains in good condition Home goods and toys ITEMS NOT ACCEPTED: Commercial loads Furniture or household goods Garbage Yard waste Household recycling \(items that go in your cart/dumpster\) Hazardous waste Automotive waste Construction waste Non-recyclable or reusable items Car seats Mattresses TO VOLUNTEER FOR THIS EVENT, PLEASE REGISTER USING ACCURATE CONTACT INFORMATION. IF YOU ARE ONLY DROPPING OFF ITEMS, NO REGISTRATION IS NEEDED.

item.title: Best Day Ever: South Park
item.description:
Saturday, March 21, 2026 at 9:30 AM

Location: South Park Hall, 1253 S Cloverdale St., Seattle, WA

Join us for our first Best Day Ever event with Cultivate South Park! This exciting one-day event will feature activities like West African dance & storytelling, chocolate tasting, Duwamish River tours, local food tasting, arts & crafts, and more. Sign up now for a full day of joy and community! Best Day Ever is a one-day, community-powered experience that celebrates the vibrancy, culture, and pride of Seattle’s neighborhoods. Co-created by the Department of Neighborhoods \(DON\) and the Seattle Chamber of Connection \(SCoC\), Best Day Ever strengthens civic belonging by bringing people, place, and purpose together. Guided by DON’s Community Engagement Framework and the Chamber’s Six Points of Connection, this initiative uplifts community leadership and honors the histories, creativity, and cultural assets that make each neighborhood unique. A thriving neighborhood is one where residents feel connected, represented, and proud of where they live — and Best Day Ever is designed to bring that feeling to life. Each activation builds belonging through service, storytelling, movement, shared meals, joyful connection, and economic visibility for local small businesses.

From SEAtoday - All Events

item.title: March Madness Watch Party at Five Iron Golf - Seattle Capitol Hill
item.description:
Saturday, March 21, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Five Iron Golf - Seattle Capitol Hill, 1525 11th Ave Suite 100, Seattle, WA

Five Iron Golf is your College Basketball HQ this March! Gather your crew and book 1–2 simulator bays for up to 12 people \(6 people per bay\) for a bracket-busting watch party experience. Enjoy all-day TV coverage of college basketball alongside cutting-edge golf and multisport simulators, a locally-inspired menu, and custom beverage packages. Book your watch party for March 19 or 20 and you'll automatically be entered to win a 20-person happy hour with drinks and appetizers — a $1,000 value! Group packages can include reserved event space, team-specific décor, a dedicated staff member, full-screen and/or picture-in-picture viewing, a customizable menu, beverage packages, free club rentals, multisport simulators, and table games. Select the "Small Event" option at booking to ensure simulator bays are placed side-by-side. Availability of select features varies by location. Promotions: Happy Hour – "It's 5i o'Clock Somewhere" \(Monday–Friday, 3–6 PM\) Enjoy $5 bottomless boneless wings, $10 bottomless bone-in wings, $5 select beers/cans/cocktails, $5 dips \(guacamole, hot honey ricotta, hummus\), half-off pizzas, and $5 featured shots. Dine-in only at participating locations; simulator rental may be required. 5i After Dark – "Swing In, For Less"  \(Sunday–Thursday 9 PM to close | Friday–Saturday 10 PM to close\) Features $29/hr simulator rentals plus the same great food and drink specials as Happy Hour, with wings priced per dozen rather than bottomless. Dine-in only at participating locations; simulator rental may be required.

item.description:

Spring Equinox is a season of dynamic balance, as we turn toward the energies of the East and all that is newly emerging in the earth and in ourselves. Sturtevant's \(part of Christy Sports\) is hosting the annual Ski-Ya-Later Sale from March 6-29, offering up to 50% off current-season skis, snowboards, boots, bindings, apparel, and more—perfect as spring storms bring late-season conditions and families plan ahead during spring break. Shop in-person at our Bellevue locations for expert tuning & repair, custom boot fitting, water sports rentals, and more: Sturtevant's – 13131 NE 20th St., Bellevue, WA 98005 \(425.869.7669\) Hours: Mon-Fri 10 am-7 pm, Sat-Sun 10 am- 6 pm Sturtevant's Ski Mart – 13219 NE 20th St., Bellevue, WA 98005 \(425.637.8958\) Hours: Mon-Fri 10 am-7 pm, Sat-Sun 10 am-6 pm Or head to Tacoma: Sturtevant's Ski Mart – 2220 So. 37th St., Tacoma, WA 98409 \(253.473.1134\) Hours: Mon-Fri 10 am-7 pm, Sat-Sun 10 am-6 pm. item.title: Homebuyer Education Workshop [VIRTUAL]
item.description:
Saturday, March 21, 2026 at 10 AM

This is a free five-hour workshop to help you meet the WA State Housing Finance Commission’s education requirements for down payment assistance and a variety of affordable housing programs and opportunities for qualified applicants! These requirements include the successful completion of a Homebuyers Education Seminar meeting the current FHA, Fannie Mae, FreddieMac, and SMBA benchmark standards.  At the conclusion of the workshop, participants will receive a certificate of achievement from the WA State Housing Finance Commission and DFI valid for two years from the date of issuance.  To join our next workshop, please complete and submit the following registration form at least 24-hours before your selected workshop date. Once this form has been submitted, be sure to check your email for a digital confirmation, as well as additional follow up and important information from a ULMS Housing Department staff member.   Virtual workshops are held every 3rd and 4th Saturday of the month from 10:00AM – 3:30PM. A Zoom link will be sent to you via a ULMS Housing department staff member upon completing registration.

item.title: South Lynnwood Park Stewardship Event
item.description:
Saturday, March 21, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Scriber Lake Park, 5562 196th St SW, Lynnwood, WA

City of Lynnwood Parks welcomes volunteers of all abilities who want to contribute to the restoration and conservation of our parks in South Lynnwood Neighborhood. The goal of each event is to provide a safe, enjoyable, and effective hands-on experience for you to help improve the health of your urban parks. These monthly events, which will be held on the third weekend of each month, will involve removing invasive plants, help with restoration plantings, and picking up garbage. Events are 100% outdoors and will generally be held ‘rain or shine’ but may be canceled or relocated if weather or site conditions are unsafe such as high winds, flooding, snow, lightning, or unsafe air quality \(smoke\).

From SEAtoday - All Events

Event details

item.title: TADAIMA: I'm Home
item.description:
Saturday, March 21, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Museum of History and Industry, 860 Terry Ave N, Seattle, Seattle, WA

After more than 80 years, a treasured collection returns “home” to Seattle’s Japanese American community. In 1942 dozens of traditional Boys’ and Girls’ Day dolls were entrusted to Bailey Gatzert Elementary School by Japanese American families facing forced incarceration. Most were never reclaimed after the war. Artist Miya Sukune uses recent research in MOHAI’s collection, as well as interviews with Japanese American survivors and their descendants, to create an installation called “Tadaima,” meaning “I’m home.” TADAIMA: “I’m Home!” Bringing Seattle’s Girls’ and Boys’ Day Dolls Home reconnects the dolls with their traditional role of protecting and blessing children. It invites visitors to celebrate the resilience, memory, and enduring traditions of the Japanese American Community.

From SEAtoday - All Events

item.title: BrainFest with Allen Institute
item.description:
Saturday, March 21, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Pacific Science Center, 200 Second Ave. North, Seattle, WA

Join us for the third annual BrainFest, a day of brainy fun for all ages at PacSci. Explore hands-on activities, meet Allen Institute scientists and collaborators, learn about cutting-edge neuroscience research, and even touch a real brain! Whether you’re a budding young scientist, a lifelong learner, or simply fascinated by how the mind works, BrainFest 2026 promises a full day of discovery, inspiration, and wonder. Tickets BrainFest is included with General Admission. A limited number of free general admission tickets will be given out on the day of the event on a first-come first-serve basis. This event is a partnership of the Allen Institute and Pacific Science Center.

Sturtevant's \(part of Christy Sports\) is hosting the annual Ski-Ya-Later Sale from March 6-29, offering up to 50% off current-season skis, snowboards, boots, bindings, apparel, and more—perfect as spring storms bring late-season conditions and families plan ahead during spring break. Shop in-person at our Bellevue locations for expert tuning & repair, custom boot fitting, water sports rentals, and more: Sturtevant's – 13131 NE 20th St., Bellevue, WA 98005 \(425.869.7669\) Hours: Mon-Fri 10 am-7 pm, Sat-Sun 10 am- 6 pm Sturtevant's Ski Mart – 13219 NE 20th St., Bellevue, WA 98005 \(425.637.8958\) Hours: Mon-Fri 10 am-7 pm, Sat-Sun 10 am-6 pm Or head to Tacoma: Sturtevant's Ski Mart – 2220 So. 37th St., Tacoma, WA 98409 \(253.473.1134\) Hours: Mon-Fri 10 am-7 pm, Sat-Sun 10 am-6 pm. Great for families: Stop by for kids' fit checks, season rental info, and early purchases before fall pricing. All discounted items are final sale; selection varies by store. Also shop online at christysports.com or sturtevants.com. Upgrade your gear locally this spring—visit Sturtevant's Bellevue or Tacoma today. Dates: March 6 – March 29, 2026 \(Progressive discounts: Up to 30% off March 6-12 with the best selection; up to 40% off March 13-19; up to 50% off March 20-29 for the deepest savings\)

item.title: South Lynnwood Park Stewardship Event
item.description:
Saturday, March 21, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Scriber Lake Park, 5562 196th St SW, Lynnwood, WA

City of Lynnwood Parks welcomes volunteers of all abilities who want to contribute to the restoration and conservation of our parks in South Lynnwood Neighborhood. The goal of each event is to provide a safe, enjoyable, and effective hands-on experience for you to help improve the health of your urban parks. These monthly events, which will be held on the third weekend of each month, will involve removing invasive plants, help with restoration plantings, and picking up garbage. Events are 100% outdoors and will generally be held ‘rain or shine’ but may be canceled or relocated if weather or site conditions are unsafe such as high winds, flooding, snow, lightning, or unsafe air quality \(smoke\).

From SEAtoday - Sports & Outdoors

Event details

item.title: Homebuyer Education Workshop [VIRTUAL]
item.description:
Saturday, March 21, 2026 at 10 AM

This is a free five-hour workshop to help you meet the WA State Housing Finance Commission’s education requirements for down payment assistance and a variety of affordable housing programs and opportunities for qualified applicants! These requirements include the successful completion of a Homebuyers Education Seminar meeting the current FHA, Fannie Mae, FreddieMac, and SMBA benchmark standards.  At the conclusion of the workshop, participants will receive a certificate of achievement from the WA State Housing Finance Commission and DFI valid for two years from the date of issuance.  To join our next workshop, please complete and submit the following registration form at least 24-hours before your selected workshop date. Once this form has been submitted, be sure to check your email for a digital confirmation, as well as additional follow up and important information from a ULMS Housing Department staff member.   Virtual workshops are held every 3rd and 4th Saturday of the month from 10:00AM – 3:30PM. A Zoom link will be sent to you via a ULMS Housing department staff member upon completing registration.

item.title: BrainFest with Allen Institute
item.description:
Saturday, March 21, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Pacific Science Center, 200 Second Ave. North, Seattle, WA

Join us for the third annual BrainFest, a day of brainy fun for all ages at PacSci. Explore hands-on activities, meet Allen Institute scientists and collaborators, learn about cutting-edge neuroscience research, and even touch a real brain! Whether you’re a budding young scientist, a lifelong learner, or simply fascinated by how the mind works, BrainFest 2026 promises a full day of discovery, inspiration, and wonder. Tickets BrainFest is included with General Admission. A limited number of free general admission tickets will be given out on the day of the event on a first-come first-serve basis. This event is a partnership of the Allen Institute and Pacific Science Center.

Event details

item.title: TADAIMA: I'm Home
item.description:
Saturday, March 21, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Museum of History and Industry, 860 Terry Ave N, Seattle, Seattle, WA

After more than 80 years, a treasured collection returns “home” to Seattle’s Japanese American community. In 1942 dozens of traditional Boys’ and Girls’ Day dolls were entrusted to Bailey Gatzert Elementary School by Japanese American families facing forced incarceration. Most were never reclaimed after the war. Artist Miya Sukune uses recent research in MOHAI’s collection, as well as interviews with Japanese American survivors and their descendants, to create an installation called “Tadaima,” meaning “I’m home.” TADAIMA: “I’m Home!” Bringing Seattle’s Girls’ and Boys’ Day Dolls Home reconnects the dolls with their traditional role of protecting and blessing children. It invites visitors to celebrate the resilience, memory, and enduring traditions of the Japanese American Community.

From SEAtoday - Community & Civic

item.description:
Sunday, March 22, 2026 at 11 AM

Location: Armory

Seattle Center Festál presents Seattle’s French Fest: A Celebration of French Speaking Cultures in partnership with France Education Northwest (FEN). The festival celebrates the diversity of traditions, ideas, dialects, and more that make up French-speaking cultures around the world. While France is often the most well-known French-speaking country for people in the U.S., there are many other countries and places all around the world where French is spoken.
